ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR PLAINTIFF LAURA WOODWARD'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES BY DEFENDANT (TENTATIVE RULING PART 1, SEE PART 2) HEARING SET FOR JAN-22-2025 AT 09:00 AM IN DEPT 301

DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO THE JAN-22-2025 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF LAURA WOODWARD'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES BY DEFENDANT; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH MODIFICATIONS. THE COURT DENIES PRODUCTION AS TO COMMUNICATIONS REGARDING THOSE CASES AS TO WHICH IT NEVER HAD AN AGREEMENT WITH THE PLAINTIFF. THE COURT ALSO NARROWS THE SCOPE OF PRODUCTION TO 15 CASES AS TO COMMUNICATIONS REGARDING THE CASES AS TO WHICH THE PLAINTIFF DOES NOT MAKE A CLAIM FOR FUNDS. THE PLAINTIFF IS TO SELECT THOSE 15 CASES. THE TENTATIVE RULING IS OTHERWISE ADOPTED (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). THE PLAINTIFF IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT AN ORDER WHICH CONFORMS WITH THE RULING, WITH A COVER LETTER OUTLINING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (302/HEK)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-21-2025 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant
Off Calendar Jan-22-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Plaintiff LAURA WOODWARD, in pro per present (tel: 415-398-8575).
Atty. JOHN BROWN, Esq. of Cornerstone Law Group (tel: 415-409-8600) for Defendant.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF LAURA WOODWARD's Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant.

Matter argued. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling with modifications. The Court denies production as to communications regarding those cases as to which it never had an agreement with the Plaintiff. The Court also narrows the scope of production to 15 cases as to communications regarding the cases as to which the Plaintiff does not make a claim for funds. The Plaintiff is to select those 15 cases. The tentative ruling is otherwise adopted:

The motion is granted in part as to Request 3. The cited discovery meets the standard for discovery relevance. The Kraus declaration shows that as presently phrased the request is unduly burdensome. (Kraus Decl., pars. 4-6.) Therefore, documents "referencing or related to" the specified estates do not include i) communications and documents where defendant attempted to locate possible estate heirs who could share in distribution of the estate's assets; ii) genealogy documents showing possible heirs and their contact information; iii) documents and correspondence with public agencies that keep vital statistics records for the purpose of locating heirs and proving heirships that can share in the estate; iv) proof of heirship document (such as birth, marriage and death records); v) defendant's communications with the estate beneficiaries, including requests for updates and other information regarding the case. If there are any responsive documents not produced on the grounds of the attorney-client privilege or work product doctrine, defendant shall provide a privilege log. (CCP 2031.240(c).) Defendant shall provide code compliant responses by February 10, 2025.

Grant as to Request 4. The documents referencing Applegate and his firms are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP 2031.240(c).) If there are any responsive documents not produced on the grounds of the attorney-client privilege or work product doctrine, defendant shall provide a privilege log. (CCP 2031.240(c).) Defendant shall provide code compliant responses by February 10, 2025.

The request for sanctions is denied because both parties acted with substantial justification.

The Plaintiff is to prepare and submit an order which conforms with the ruling, with a cover letter outlining compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/HEK)

Notice Of Re-Set Hearing Date For Plaintiff Motion For Relief Purusant To Ccp Section 128.7 Agaunst Defendant Legacy And Its Attorneys John C. Brona And Cornerstone Law Group
Off Calendar Dec-10-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF LAURA WOODWARD'S Motion For Relief Purusant To CCP Section 128.7 Against Defendant Legacy and its Attorneys John C. Brona and Cornerstone Law Group.

Plaintiff's motion for sanctions is denied.

Plaintiff failed to comply with the 21-day safe harbor periods of CCP 128.5(f)(1)(B) or CCP 128.7(c)(1). Plaintiff served the motion on September 20, 2024, and the motion to continue trial was granted on October 8, 2024. (Broadcast Music, Inc. v. Structured Asset Sales, LLC (2022) 75 Cal.App.5th 596, 605 [[I]f the objectionable document is resolved by the court during the safe harbor period, sanctions cannot be awarded.] The court further notes that plaintiff withdrew the motion to deem on September 26, 2024.

In any event, this motion substantively fails. Plaintiff's belief that defendant's declarations contain inadmissible evidence is a basis for an evidentiary objection, not sanctions. Our adversary system requires that attorneys and litigants be provided substantial breathing room to develop and assert factual and legal arguments. (Peake v. Underwood (2014) 227 Cal.App.4th 428, 433.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (301)
